params.description: Disable QUIC/HTTP3 in Chrome on test PC (cycle 94 H3 bypass mitigation)
params.context: Cycle 94 R-21: APF intercept layer diagnosis. mistral.ai and you.com advertise alt-svc: h3=:443 in their HTTPS responses, causing Chrome to switch to UDP/443 (HTTP/3) for subsequent requests. APF only inspects TCP/TLS — UDP/443 packets bypass entirely (verified: 0 QUIC events in /var/log/etap.log on Etap server even though block_quic=true config + L7 detection are correct in engine — UDP traffic doesn't reach inline path). Workaround: disable QUIC in Chrome on test PC so all traffic stays on TCP/H2 → APF intercept works as designed.

steps.note: Find the 'Experimental QUIC protocol' or 'Enable experimental QUIC' flag (search bar can help: type 'quic' in the flags page search if not visible immediately)
Click the dropdown next to the flag and select 'Disabled' (default may be 'Default' which lets Chrome decide; we want explicit 'Disabled')

steps.target: Relaunch button at bottom of chrome://flags page (appears after changing any flag)
steps.note: This restarts Chrome with the new setting

steps.check: Re-open chrome://flags/#enable-quic and confirm flag value is Disabled

Open this URL with DevTools Network tab visible. Send any request. Check that the Protocol column shows h2 (NOT h3-29 / h3 / Q050 / Q046). Take screenshot 'protocol_h2_confirm.png' showing Network panel with protocol column.

params.success_criteria: QUIC flag = Disabled (verified after relaunch) AND chat.mistral.ai requests show Protocol=h2 in DevTools Network tab
params.rollback_instructions: To revert: chrome://flags/#enable-quic → set to Default → relaunch

notes: Cycle 94 R-21 disable Chrome QUIC on test PC. Required for APF intercept verification of mistral / you / any future H3-advertising AI service. Reversible via chrome://flags.
